decisions.action: MARK AS FAILING: Current content/distribution tactics are not driving repo adoption.
decisions.reason: Codeberg repo adoption flat across 9 samples (stars +0, watchers +0, forks +0)
decisions.repair: Replace stale owned-content-first repair. Hold homepage/owned-content volume at maintenance, push Codeberg-first conversion assets and third-party proof, and use only posting channels with current runtime proof instead of assuming a single default publication surface.

decisions.reason: Best avg views: 133.0 — lean into what's working.

decisions.action: Shift one future slot away from usecase toward seo-guide.
decisions.reason: seo-guide outperforms usecase on avg views.

decisions.action: Build backlinks — submit to directories and pursue guest post opportunities.
decisions.reason: Zero backlinks detected. Link acquisition is the highest-leverage SEO activity right now.

decisions.action: Build backlinks: submit to directories, guest post, earn citations
decisions.reason: Identified by daily SEO analysis as a top priority.

decisions.action: Hold Telegraph at maintenance only; do not treat more owned-content volume as the next best move.
decisions.reason: Primary repo adoption is flat and owned-content output is already saturated; shift effort to curator, backlink, and comparison distribution lanes.

decisions.action: Ship comparison-led backlink outreach packets whenever the curator queue is already full.
decisions.reason: A follow-through note is not enough when the queue is saturated; the loop needs a fresh executable distribution asset tied to existing comparison pages.

blocked_channels.reddit: ARCHITECTURALLY RETIRED 2026-05-28 — IP-blocked at Hetzner Helsinki, Tor also blocked, no proxy path. All Reddit scripts exit immediately. Active channel: GitHub Discussions.

priority_actions: Build backlinks: submit to directories, guest post, earn citations

findings: Codeberg, the primary repo, has shown no star/watch/fork movement across the recent measurement window.
Codeberg remains the stronger adoption surface and should stay the primary evaluation target.
PyPI has 1303 downloads/month (36/day) — real usage signal that repo metrics don't capture.
next_focus: Treat tactics that did not move Codeberg adoption as failing until they produce a measurable delta.

walph_workflow_hn_post.title: Show HN: Ralph Workflow - Simple Agent-Agnostic AI Orchestrator based on Ralph.

shortlisted_opportunities.name: CCW (Claude Code Workflow) by sermakarevich
shortlisted_opportunities.relevance: HIGH - Direct competitor. Workflow generator for Claude Code using SDD approach. Show HN 2026-05-28.
shortlisted_opportunities.ralph_angle: Spec-driven development workflow - directly comparable to Ralph's loop-based spec-driven approach. Discusses multi-step task orchestration with clear handoffs.
shortlisted_opportunities.action: Write comparison page: Ralph Workflow vs CCW. Highlight that CCW is Claude-Code-specific while Ralph is agent-agnostic.

shortlisted_opportunities.name: BetterClaw
shortlisted_opportunities.relevance: HIGH - Mentions OpenClaw explicitly, workflow tooling in same space. Show HN 2026-05-01.
shortlisted_opportunities.ralph_angle: Paragraph→graph workflow compiler for gating agent tools. Asks 'what other agent runtimes beyond Claude Code + Cowork + OpenClaw?'
shortlisted_opportunities.action: Comment on BetterClaw thread suggesting Ralph Workflow as an additional runtime. Write blog post: 'Why BetterClaw + Ralph Workflow is a powerful combination for agent safety.'

shortlisted_opportunities.name: Stoneforge
shortlisted_opportunities.relevance: HIGH - Direct orchestrator competitor. Open-source parallel AI coding agent orchestration. Show HN 2026-03-05.
shortlisted_opportunities.ralph_angle: Director agent / worktree isolation pattern is different from Ralph's loop-based approach. Stoneforge is opinionated about agent architecture; Ralph is not.
shortlisted_opportunities.action: Write comparison: Ralph Workflow vs Stoneforge - loop-based vs task-queue-based orchestration. Emphasize Ralph's simplicity advantage.

shortlisted_opportunities.name: PocketOS Database Deletion Incident (April 25)
shortlisted_opportunities.relevance: HIGH - Agent deleted production database via Cursor/Claude. Major safety discussion driving demand for guardrails.
shortlisted_opportunities.ralph_angle: Ralph's review-loop architecture is already designed to catch destructive actions before they execute. Ralph's verification step naturally provides the safety layer that BetterClaw and others are building externally.
shortlisted_opportunities.action: Write blog post: 'How loop-based agent orchestration prevents the next PocketOS incident.' Emphasize disciplined review as a built-in safety pattern.

shortlisted_opportunities.name: Corral
shortlisted_opportunities.relevance: MEDIUM-HIGH - Open-source orchestration layer for AI coding agents. pip install agent-corral. Show HN 2026-03-06.
shortlisted_opportunities.ralph_angle: Parallel git worktrees + tmux. Dashboard for tracking agents. Different approach from Ralph's sequential loop structure.

shortlisted_opportunities.name: Forge
shortlisted_opportunities.relevance: MEDIUM - Deterministic orchestrator - tests as hard gates. Show HN 2026-02-19.
shortlisted_opportunities.ralph_angle: Similar philosophy - deterministic agent orchestration with verification gates. Provider-agnostic like Ralph.
shortlisted_opportunities.action: Write lightweight comparison. Could use Forge's 'code decides if it ships' language as inspiration.

shortlisted_opportunities.name: Resumable Sub-agents in Claude Code v2.0.60
shortlisted_opportunities.relevance: MEDIUM - Important ecosystem development. Resumable sub-agents reduce need for external orchestration at the basic level.
shortlisted_opportunities.ralph_angle: Claude Code is absorbing some orchestration features internally. Framing: Ralph provides the multi-agent orchestration layer that Claude Code doesn't.

shortlisted_opportunities.name: Autonomous Research Swarm (Planner/Worker/Judge)
shortlisted_opportunities.relevance: MEDIUM - Inspired by Cursor's scaling-agents blog post. Repo-as-shared-memory approach.
shortlisted_opportunities.ralph_angle: Planner/Worker/Judge pattern is similar to Ralph's Plan/Build/Verify loops. Cite as validation of loop-based architecture.

shortlisted_opportunities.name: Claude Code Workflow generator (CCW) - SDD plugins
shortlisted_opportunities.relevance: MEDIUM - Shows demand for spec-driven workflow tooling. Also an SDD approach.
shortlisted_opportunities.ralph_angle: Second SDD workflow tool found. The spec-driven approach is being validated by multiple independent builders.

shortlisted_opportunities.name: AgentClick - Human-in-loop review for AI coding agents
shortlisted_opportunities.relevance: LOWER but notable - Human-in-the-loop review UI for AI coding agents.
shortlisted_opportunities.ralph_angle: Complementary tool. Could integrate Ralph's review output into AgentClick's review UI.

rejected_candidates: Pitaya (orchestrator, old - Aug 2025)
Diraigent (self-hosted orchestration, low engagement)
Ctxbin (agent handoff CLI, narrow tool)
Mesa (multi-agent code review, competing but different tier)
AgentPort (agent security gateway, adjacent)
Shellfirm (safety guardrails, low engagement)
Yaw (terminal built around AI CLI, not an orchestrator)
Autofix Bot (DeepSource, code review only)
Claude × Codex Collab (two agents, very specific)
ML-Ralph (different domain - ML experiments)
Pickle Rick port (Ralph Wiggum technique, old - Feb 2026)
BMAD-METHOD integration (advertorial, not an orchestrator)
Ask HN agent safety (user support thread, not actionable)
Claude Code to automate work (general Q&A thread)
Scalable agentic workflow frameworks (old thread)
Multi AI agent orchestration frameworks x2 (old threads)
How I code 10x faster x3 (general LLM comparison threads)
Integrating BMAD-METHOD (marketing post)
Content pipeline with Claude Code (different use case)
Best coding practices unattended bots (UiPath, irrelevant)
Super confused agent frameworks (old, low value)

existing_competitors_confirmed: Stoneforge - parallel orchestration, worktrees, Director/Worker/Steward pattern
Conductor OSS - enterprise orchestration, durable execution
competitive_landscape_update.category_observation: The AI coding agent orchestration space is rapidly heating up. At least 6+ open-source orchestrator tools launched in the last 6 months. This validates the category but increases competition for developer mindshare.

pain_points: Context window degradation on long runs
Agent safety / production database deletion fears
Lack of structured handoffs between agents
ralph_fit_signals: Loop-based architecture directly addresses context-window degradation (clear restart points)
Verification loop addresses the safety concern highlighted by PocketOS incident
Composable loop structure maps to the Planner/Worker/Judge pattern being validated by Cursor et al.
Spec-driven approach aligns with the SDD trend (multiple tools adopting this)

content_opportunities.urgency: HIGH - Current events (PocketOS incident) make this timely

reddit_reply_opportunities.reason: Reddit direct fetch is blocked (403). No recent content could be inspected for reply opportunities.

market_intelligence.search_provider_degradation_note: DuckDuckGo search is currently returning bot-detection challenges. Reddit direct fetches are blocked entirely (403/block). HN Algolia API worked well and provided most of today's data.

distribution_lane.reason: At least one curator outreach review window is now due, so the highest-leverage move is a concrete follow-up packet instead of another reset or measurement hold.
reasons: Primary Codeberg adoption is flat in the current measurement window.
1 live external marketing action(s) already shipped in the last 6 hours.
Reddit execution is fail-closed from this environment right now, so the loop should not treat another Reddit pass as a shippable distribution lane.
HN/Lobsters has repeated as a blocked ceiling, so the loop should create a different distribution lane in the same run.
2 curator outreach targets are already live in the queue, so the loop should advance or review them instead of regenerating the same packet.
6 curator targets are already inside active reply/backlink review windows, so another same-family outreach batch would mostly create unmeasurable overlap.
2 prepared curator targets still need a canonical execution handoff packet.

Curator follow-ups are now due for 5 target(s) (filipecalegario/awesome-vibe-coding, QAInsights/awesome-ai-tools, 0xWelt/Awesome-Vibe-Coding), so the loop should not hide behind another measurement hold when real follow-through is ready.
Manual-contact-only curator targets are still waiting for execution (vivy-yi/awesome-agent-orchestration), so the loop should use the existing contact-discovery asset before inventing new reset work.

Some remaining publisher targets only expose non-runtime-executable channels (ctxt.dev / Signum, TLDL, ComputingForGeeks), so they should not keep this lane looking actionable until a sendable path exists.

Primary-repo-flat repair already surfaced fresh developer-native publishers with public contact paths (AXME Code, WyeWorks, Bollwerk / Werkstatt), so the loop should package that Codeberg-first outreach instead of ending at measurement hold.
The comparison/backlink queue is already fully prepared, but GitHub auth is blocked here, so that lane is manual-only follow-through rather than fresh live outbound work.
Curator outreach already has enough live measurement windows open; the next move should create fresh demand capture instead of piling on more curator contact.
Backlink status already shows 3 live directory listing(s), so the loop should reuse that evidence instead of acting like every submission is still opaque.

A current directory secondary-surface repair packet already exists for a live page that still misroutes or obscures Codeberg repo intent, so the loop should reuse that asset instead of calling the board empty.
The prior StackOverflow draft pass returned zero candidates, so if that lane is chosen it must rely on the repaired API-driven search rather than the old scrape-only path.
A fresh StackOverflow answer draft already exists, so do not rerun the same demand-capture lane until that asset is posted, reused, or ages out of the current review window.
The StackOverflow handoff packet is already current, so regenerating it again would be fake progress.
shared_findings_used: adoption_metrics_latest.json: Codeberg movement is the primary success gate
outreach-log.md: avoid duplicate submission work and repeated HN/Lobsters-only handoff
market_intelligence_latest.json: reusable competitor comparisons and positioning truths
